### API Integration Tool Example

Source: https://github.com/composiohq/awesome-claude-skills/blob/master/mcp-builder/reference/mcp_best_practices.md

Example of a tool that wraps an external API to create GitHub issues.

```typescript
{
  name: "github_create_issue",
  description: "Create a GitHub issue",
  inputSchema: {
    type: "object",
    properties: {
      title: { type: "string" },
      body: { type: "string" },
      labels: { type: "array", items: { type: "string" } }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Implementing Tools in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/composiohq/awesome-claude-skills/blob/master/mcp-builder/reference/mcp_best_practices.md

Example of setting up a server and defining tools in TypeScript. Includes request handlers for listing tools and executing them.

```typescript
const server = new Server({
  name: "example-server",
  version: "1.0.0"
}, {
  capabilities: {
    tools: {}
  }
});

// Define available tools
server.setRequestHandler(ListToolsRequestSchema, async () => {
  return {
    tools: [{
      name: "calculate_sum",
      description: "Add two numbers together",
      inputSchema: {
        type: "object",
        properties: {
          a: { type: "number" },
          b: { type: "number" }
        },
        required: ["a", "b"]
      }
    }]
  };
});

// Handle tool execution
server.setRequestHandler(CallToolRequestSchema, async (request) => {
  if (request.params.name === "calculate_sum") {
    const { a, b } = request.params.arguments;
    return {
      content: [
        {
          type: "text",
          text: String(a + b)
        }
      ]
    };
  }
  throw new Error("Tool not found");
});
```

--------------------------------

### Render Text with Outline using Typography

Source: https://github.com/composiohq/awesome-claude-skills/blob/master/slack-gif-creator/SKILL.md

Shows how to render text with an outline for improved readability, especially in small GIFs like emojis. This function takes a frame, text content, position, font size, text color, outline color, and outline width as input.

```python
from core.typography import draw_text_with_outline, TYPOGRAPHY_SCALE

# Text with outline (helps readability)
draw_text_with_outline(
    frame, "BONK!",
    position=(240, 100),
    font_size=TYPOGRAPHY_SCALE['h1'],  # 60px
    text_color=(255, 68, 68),
    outline_color=(0, 0, 0),
    outline_width=4,
    centered=True
)
```

--------------------------------

### Create Goal Animation with Impact Flash and Text (Python)

Source: https://github.com/composiohq/awesome-claude-skills/blob/master/slack-gif-creator/SKILL.md

Generates a GIF of a soccer goal celebration. It creates a gradient background, adds an impact flash on early frames, draws a soccer ball emoji, and displays 'GOAL!' text after the initial frames. Assumes functions like `create_gradient_background`, `create_impact_flash`, `draw_emoji_enhanced`, and `draw_text_with_outline` are available.

```python
for i in range(8):
    frame = create_gradient_background(480, 480, (240, 248, 255), (200, 230, 255))

    # Flash on first frames
    if i < 3:
        frame = create_impact_flash(frame, (240, 350), radius=120, intensity=0.6)

    draw_emoji_enhanced(frame, '⚽', position=(220, 350), size=80)

    # Text appears
    if i > 2:
        draw_text_with_outline(frame, "GOAL!", position=(240, 150),
                              font_size=60, text_color=(255, 68, 68),
                              outline_color=(0, 0, 0), outline_width=4, centered=True)

    builder.add_frame(frame)

builder.save('goal.gif', num_colors=128)
```

--------------------------------

### Presentation Slide and Shape Formatting Options

Source: https://github.com/composiohq/awesome-claude-skills/blob/master/document-skills/pptx/SKILL.md

This JSON snippet illustrates various formatting properties that can be applied to presentation slides and shapes. It includes options for alignment, spacing, font styles, and colors, demonstrating how to define these properties for text elements within a presentation.

```json
{
  "alignment": "CENTER",    // CENTER, RIGHT (not LEFT)
  "space_before": 10.0,     // space before paragraph in points
  "space_after": 6.0,       // space after paragraph in points
  "line_spacing": 22.4,     // line spacing in points
  "font_name": "Arial",     // from first run
  "font_size": 14.0,        // in points
  "bold": true,
  "italic": false,
  "underline": false,
  "color": "FF0000"         // RGB color
}
```

--------------------------------

### Apply Visual Effects with Visual Effects Module

Source: https://github.com/composiohq/awesome-claude-skills/blob/master/slack-gif-creator/SKILL.md

Demonstrates the use of visual effects like particle systems (sparkles, confetti), impact flashes, and shockwave rings to add impact to GIFs. It involves initializing particle systems, emitting particles, updating their state, rendering them onto a frame, and applying flash or shockwave effects.

```python
from core.visual_effects import ParticleSystem, create_impact_flash, create_shockwave_rings

# Particle system
particles = ParticleSystem()
particles.emit_sparkles(x=240, y=200, count=15)
particles.emit_confetti(x=240, y=200, count=20)

# Update and render each frame
particles.update()
particles.render(frame)

# Flash effect
frame = create_impact_flash(frame, position=(240, 200), radius=100)

# Shockwave rings
frame = create_shockwave_rings(frame, position=(240, 200), radii=[30, 60, 90])
```

--------------------------------

### Data Processing Tool Example

Source: https://github.com/composiohq/awesome-claude-skills/blob/master/mcp-builder/reference/mcp_best_practices.md

Example of a tool that analyzes CSV files with specified operations like sum, average, or count.

```typescript
{
  name: "analyze_csv",
  description: "Analyze a CSV file",
  inputSchema: {
    type: "object",
    properties: {
      filepath: { type: "string" },
      operations: {
        type: "array",
        items: {
          enum: ["sum", "average", "count"]
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Example JSON Structure for Replacing Presentation Text

Source: https://github.com/composiohq/awesome-claude-skills/blob/master/document-skills/pptx/SKILL.md

This JSON structure demonstrates how to format replacement text for presentation slides and shapes. It shows how to specify paragraphs with various formatting options like text, alignment, bolding, bullets, and color, and clarifies that shapes not listed in this JSON will have their text cleared.

```json
{
  "slide-0": {
    "shape-0": {
      "paragraphs": [
        {
          "text": "New presentation title text",
          "alignment": "CENTER",
          "bold": true
        },
        {
          "text": "Section Header",
          "bold": true
        },
        {
          "text": "First bullet point without bullet symbol",
          "bullet": true,
          "level": 0
        },
        {
          "text": "Red colored text",
          "color": "FF0000"
        },
        {
          "text": "Theme colored text",
          "theme_color": "DARK_1"
        },
        {
          "text": "Regular paragraph text without special formatting"
        }
      ]
    }
    // shape-1 and shape-2 from inventory will be cleared automatically
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Get Color Palette with Color Palettes Module

Source: https://github.com/composiohq/awesome-claude-skills/blob/master/slack-gif-creator/SKILL.md

Illustrates how to retrieve pre-defined color palettes for creating professional-looking GIFs. It takes a palette name (e.g., 'vibrant', 'pastel') and returns a dictionary containing colors like background, primary, and accent.

```python
from core.color_palettes import get_palette

# Get a pre-made palette
palette = get_palette('vibrant')  # or 'pastel', 'dark', 'neon', 'professional'

bg_color = palette['background']
text_color = palette['primary']
accent_color = palette['accent']
```

--------------------------------

### Compose Animation Primitives in Python

Source: https://github.com/composiohq/awesome-claude-skills/blob/master/slack-gif-creator/SKILL.md

Example of combining multiple animation effects (bounce + shake). Demonstrates frame-by-frame composition.

```python
for i in range(num_frames):
    frame = create_blank_frame(480, 480, bg_color)

    # Bounce motion
    t_bounce = i / (num_frames - 1)
    y = interpolate(start_y, ground_y, t_bounce, 'bounce_out')

    # Add shake on impact (when y reaches ground)
    if y >= ground_y - 5:
        shake_x = math.sin(i * 2) * 10
        x = center_x + shake_x
    else:
        x = center_x

    draw_emoji(frame, '⚽', (x, y), size=60)
    builder.add_frame(frame)
```

--------------------------------

### Build and Optimize GIF with GIFBuilder

Source: https://github.com/composiohq/awesome-claude-skills/blob/master/slack-gif-creator/SKILL.md

Demonstrates how to use the GIFBuilder class to create a GIF by adding frames and saving it with specified optimization settings like color quantization and emoji optimization. It requires a GIFBuilder instance and a list of frames.

```python
from core.gif_builder import GIFBuilder

# Create builder with your chosen settings
builder = GIFBuilder(width=480, height=480, fps=20)

# Add frames (however you created them)
for frame in my_frames:
    builder.add_frame(frame)

# Save with optimization
builder.save('output.gif',
             num_colors=128,
             optimize_for_emoji=False)
```

--------------------------------

### Create Simple Reaction GIF (Pulsing Emoji)

Source: https://github.com/composiohq/awesome-claude-skills/blob/master/slack-gif-creator/SKILL.md

An example of creating a simple reaction GIF with a pulsing emoji. It uses GIFBuilder to create frames where an emoji's scale is animated using a sine wave, and then saves the GIF with emoji optimization settings. It also includes validation for Slack size.

```python
builder = GIFBuilder(128, 128, 10)

for i in range(12):
    frame = Image.new('RGB', (128, 128), (240, 248, 255))

    # Pulsing scale
    scale = 1.0 + math.sin(i * 0.5) * 0.15
    size = int(60 * scale)

    draw_emoji_enhanced(frame, '😱', position=(64-size//2, 64-size//2),
                       size=size, shadow=False)
    builder.add_frame(frame)

builder.save('reaction.gif', num_colors=40, optimize_for_emoji=True)

# Validate
from core.validators import check_slack_size
check_slack_size('reaction.gif', is_emoji=True)
```

--------------------------------

### Compose Frames with Frame Composer Utilities

Source: https://github.com/composiohq/awesome-claude-skills/blob/master/slack-gif-creator/SKILL.md

Presents utilities for basic frame composition, including creating gradient backgrounds and drawing enhanced emojis with shadows. These functions help in building visual elements for GIFs.

```python
from core.frame_composer import (
    create_gradient_background,  # Gradient backgrounds
    draw_emoji_enhanced,         # Emoji with optional shadow
    draw_circle_with_shadow,     # Shapes with depth
    draw_star                    # 5-pointed stars
)

# Gradient background
frame = create_gradient_background(480, 480, top_color, bottom_color)

# Emoji with shadow
draw_emoji_enhanced(frame, '🎉', position=(200, 200), size=80, shadow=True)
```

--------------------------------

### Implementing Tools in Python

Source: https://github.com/composiohq/awesome-claude-skills/blob/master/mcp-builder/reference/mcp_best_practices.md

Example of setting up a server and defining tools in Python. Includes decorators for listing tools and executing them.

```python
app = Server("example-server")

@app.list_tools()
async def list_tools() -> list[types.Tool]:
    return [
        types.Tool(
            name="calculate_sum",
            description="Add two numbers together",
            inputSchema={
                "type": "object",
                "properties": {
                    "a": {"type": "number"},
                    "b": {"type": "number"}
                },
                "required": ["a", "b"]
            }
        )
    ]

@app.call_tool()
async def call_tool(
    name: str,
    arguments: dict
) -> list[types.TextContent | types.ImageContent | types.EmbeddedResource]:
    if name == "calculate_sum":
        a = arguments["a"]
        b = arguments["b"]
        result = a + b
        return [types.TextContent(type="text", text=str(result))]
    raise ValueError(f"Tool not found: {name}")
```

--------------------------------

### Create Kaleidoscope Effects in Python

Source: https://github.com/composiohq/awesome-claude-skills/blob/master/slack-gif-creator/SKILL.md

Generates kaleidoscope and mirror effects with configurable segments and rotation. Requires templates.kaleidoscope module.

```python
kaleido_frame = apply_kaleidoscope(frame, segments=8)
```

```python
frames = create_kaleidoscope_animation(
    base_frame=my_frame,
    num_frames=30,
    segments=8,
    rotation_speed=1.0
)
```

```python
from templates.kaleidoscope import apply_simple_mirror

mirrored = apply_simple_mirror(frame, mode='quad')
```

--------------------------------

### Create Slide Animations in Python

Source: https://github.com/composiohq/awesome-claude-skills/blob/master/slack-gif-creator/SKILL.md

Generates sliding animations with direction control and overshoot options. Supports multiple objects. Requires templates.slide module.

```python
frames = create_slide_animation(
    direction='left',
    slide_type='in',
    overshoot=True
)
```

```python
frames = create_slide_animation(direction='left', slide_type='across')
```

```python
objects = [
    {'data': {'emoji': '🎯', 'size': 60}, 'direction': 'left', 'final_pos': (120, 240)},
    {'data': {'emoji': '🎪', 'size': 60}, 'direction': 'right', 'final_pos': (240, 240)}
]
frames = create_multi_slide(objects, stagger_delay=5)
```
